Escape to Clarity

Back to Search

Writing For Clarity

10.01.2023 - 4 ' MINUTES personal-development

Writing is not just a tool to convey information, but a means of storytelling, where the writer has the power to transport the reader to a new world of ideas, perspectives, and experiences. Whether you are writing a technical document, an academic essay, or a personal reflection, writing gives you the chance to take your readers on a journey, to share with them your thoughts, insights, and perspectives. In this note, you will find a summary of the classic writing guide The Elements of Style and a simple guide on writing for different purposes. From my personal experience, writing is a lot of rewriting.

  • The Elements of Style
    • Emphasis: Highlight important features and functions, using bold or italic text
  • Writing Guide for Different Purposes
    • Copy Writing
    • Workplace or Internal Communications
    • Personal Writing

The Elements of Style #

The Elements of Style is a classic writing guide first published in 1918 by William Strunk Jr. and later expanded by E.B. White. The elements of good writing that where discussed in the book are the following:

  1. Clarity: Clear and straightforward language, subject performing action
The app allows users to send a message by tapping the ‘Send‘ button.
  1. Concision: Keep sentences brief and to the point, using only necessary words
To add a new contact, go to the ‘Contacts‘ section and tap ‘Add Contact.‘
  1. Grammar: Ensure correct sentence structure, spelling, and punctuation
The app requires an internet connection to function.
  1. Active Voice: Use active voice to clearly convey who or what is performing the action
The app saves messages automatically.
  1. Vocabulary: Use appropriate vocabulary, avoiding jargon and complex words
The app stores messages in the 'Inbox' section.
  1. Unity: Organize the technical documentation into a logical structure, with related information grouped together
To send a message, follow these steps:
1. Open the app
2. Tap 'New Message'
3. Type the message
4. Tap 'Send.'"
  1. Coherence: Maintain a consistent tone throughout the technical documentation, avoiding abrupt changes in style or subject matter
The app's 'Settings' section allows users to adjust their notification preferences.

Emphasis: Highlight important features and functions, using bold or italic text #

The app's Settings section allows users to adjust their notification preferences.

Writing Guide for Different Purposes #

Here I came up a simple guide based on my personal copy writing, academic and personal experience.

Copy Writing #

Technical Documentation

  • Objective: Provide clear and concise instructions about the product
  • Tone: Impartial and precise
  • Language: Precise and concise language, with technical terms where necessary
  • Evidence: Graphs, diagrams, and concrete examples
  • Audience: Engineers, scientists, technicians, specialists

Email Marketing Content

  • Objective: Persuade and influence potential customers to take a specific action
  • Tone: Engaging, persuasive, and upbeat
  • Language: Conversational and attention-grabbing
  • Evidence: Benefits, testimonials, statistics, and emotional appeals
  • Audience: Prospective customers, visitors, investors, or stakeholders

Landing Pages

  • Objective: Convert website visitors into customers or leads by focusing on benefits and outcomes, rather than features
  • Tone: Persuasive, consistent with the brand’s overall messaging and style
  • Language: Easy to understand
  • Evidence: Customer testimonials, product specifications, case studies, or statistics.
  • Audience: Website visitors, general web surfers

Social Media

  • Objective: Engage and connect with followers, build brand awareness.
  • Tone: Conversational, friendly, and relatable
  • Language: Short and concise, with the use of emojis, hashtags, and attention-grabbing headlines
  • Evidence: Images, videos, and user-generated content
  • Audience: Followers, fans, customers on social media platforms

Workplace or Internal Communications #

Business Data Reporting

  • Objective: present data-driven insights and recommendations to support business decision-making
  • Tone: Clear, concise, and business-oriented
  • Language: Clear and straightforward using the common vocabulary
  • Evidence: Relies on company data and statistical analysis with visualizations and tables to illustrate findings

Cover Letters

  • Objective: Introduce yourself to an employer and convince them to consider you for a job opening
  • Tone: Professional yet personable
  • Language: Clear and concise, with an emphasis on demonstrating your qualifications for the job
  • Evidence: Provide concrete evidence of your qualifications and experience
  • Audience: Hiring managers, so keep in mind their needs, expectations, and priorities

Academic Writing

Data Science Report

  • Objective: Present technical findings and insights from data analysis, often as part of a research project or experimentation
  • Tone: Neutral, objective, and scientific
  • Language: Technical and precise, with the use of mathematical and statistical concepts
  • Evidence: Relies on data, statistical analysis, technical algorithms and techniques; The report should present the methods used and the results obtained, along with their interpretation and limitations
  • Audience: Scholars, researchers, educators, and students

Academic Essay

  • Objective: Present a stance on a specific topic based on evidence and research
  • Tone: Neutral, objective, and persuasive
  • Language: Academic, formal, and persuasive
  • Evidence: Primary and secondary sources to convince the reader of the writer’s POV
  • General Audience: Scholars, researchers, educators, and students

Academic Research

  • Objective: Present research findings or scholarly opinions
  • Tone: Neutral, objective, and scientific
  • Language: Academic, precise, third-person perspective
  • Evidence: Primary and secondary sources, including experiments, surveys, interviews, and existing research
  • Audience: Scholars, researchers, educators, and students

Academic Review

  • Objective: Critically evaluate a book or publication and provide an overview of its strengths and weaknesses
  • Tone: Formal and impartial
  • Language: Technical and academic vocabulary, third-person perspective
  • Evidence: Quotes, paraphrases, and summaries from the book being reviewed, as well as secondary sources
  • Audience: Scholars, researchers, educators, and students

Personal Writing #

Creative Writing

  • Objective: Express imagination and originality through writing.
  • Tone: Varies, can be whimsical, serious, or imaginative
  • Language: Evocative and imaginative, can use figurative language, and unconventional narrative structures
  • Evidence: Unique perspectives, vivid descriptions, and imaginative scenarios.
  • Audience: General readers, fans, admirers of creative writing

Personal Reflection

  • Objective: Explore personal thoughts and experiences
  • Tone: Personal and subjective
  • Language: First-person perspective, more conversational
  • Evidence: Personal anecdotes, feelings, and insights
  • Audience: Local readers, friends or family
